name: Circular route from Trebujena through the Campiña de Jérez
name_original: Ruta circular desde Trebujena por la Campiña de Jérez
region_intro: Road cycling around Trebujena features diverse terrain, characterized by rolling hills covered in vineyards and flat marshlands. The region is situated near the mouth of the Guadalquivir River, with its unique ecosystem of wetlands. This topography provides varied road cycling routes, ranging from gentle slopes through agricultural landscapes to more challenging paths. Best road cycling routes around Trebujena The most popular road cycling route is Circular route from Trebujena through the Campiña de Jérez, a 29.7 miles (47.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the agricultural landscapes of the Campiña de Jérez. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Lebrija to Gibalbín – circular via El Cuervo de Sevilla, a moderate 36.9 miles (59.5 km) path. This route connects towns and offers views of the surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Quiet Country Road – Castillo de Melgarejo loop from Lebrija, a 53.8 miles (86.6 km) trail leading through quiet country roads and historical landmarks, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Road cycling around Trebujena is defined by vineyard-covered hills, tranquil marshlands, and routes along the Guadalquivir River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Trebujena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Trebujena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Trebujena?
A: There are 15 road cycling routes around Trebujena, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Trebujena?
A: Road cycling around Trebujena features diverse terrain. You'll find routes with rolling hills covered in vineyards, offering scenic climbs, as well as flatter paths through the marshlands near the Guadalquivir River. This mix provides both challenging and tranquil riding experiences.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Trebujena offers 5 easy road cycling routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979454047/circular-route-from-trebujena-through-the-campina-de-jerez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular route from Trebujena through the Campiña de Jérez&lt;/a&gt;, which is 29.7 miles (47.9 km) long and explores agricultural landscapes.

Q: Where can I find more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For more advanced cyclists, there are 3 difficult routes around Trebujena. One challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19321744" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos Dam – Puerta Matrera loop from Lebrija&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 94.3 km and features significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Trebujena?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979454047/circular-route-from-trebujena-through-the-campina-de-jerez" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular route from Trebujena through the Campiña de Jérez&lt;/a&gt;, which starts and ends in Trebujena. Another is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995749318/from-lebrija-to-gibalbin-circular-via-el-cuervo-de-sevilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Lebrija to Gibalbín – circular via El Cuervo de Sevilla&lt;/a&gt;, offering a loop through towns and countryside.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer beautiful views, especially over the vineyard-covered hills and the Guadalquivir River. The Castillo del Altozano in Trebujena provides panoramic views of the surrounding natural landscape, including the marshes and the river. Sunsets over the Guadalquivir with Doñana in the background are particularly renowned.

Q: What attractions can I see or visit near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the cycling, you can explore the charming urban center of Trebujena with historical sites like the Diocesan Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Palomares and the Castillo del Altozano. Along the routes, you might encounter natural highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doñana Marshes&lt;/a&gt; or scenic stretches like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5258049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road between the fields and wind turbines&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Trebujena?
A: The road cycling routes in Trebujena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain, from quiet country roads through vineyards to tranquil marshland paths, and the opportunity to explore the region's unique natural beauty.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a quiet cycling experience with less traffic?
A: Yes, the region is known for routes with little traffic, providing a very quiet and relaxing ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6880338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quiet Country Road – Castillo de Melgarejo loop from Lebrija&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through peaceful rural areas and historical landmarks.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Trebujena?
A: Trebujena offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. The mild winters make it suitable for year-round riding, while spring and autumn provide comfortable temperatures for exploring the vineyards and marshlands.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other activities in Trebujena?
A: Absolutely! The area's proximity to the Guadalquivir River and the Marismas de Trebujena makes it excellent for birdwatching. You can also enjoy local gastronomy, especially the young wine (must) from the vineyards, or take a ferry across to the Doñana Natural Park from nearby Sanlúcar de Barrameda.
tips: Plaza in memory of Federico García Lorca, a Spanish poet, playwright and prose writer, assassinated at the beginning of the Spanish civil war. The square has the seated statue of the poet.
Definition of the fields of Cádiz - a long flat road and views of modern windmills. There is no hard shoulder but not much traffic either.

waypoints.name: Road between the fields and wind turbines
